title: Double merge node halts workflow execution
symptom: Using two merge nodes in a workflow causes it to halt unexpectedly during execution. The workflow stops at the start of the second loop.
applies_when: Workflow is expected to continue looping
not_this_if: Only one merge node is used
root_cause: The merge node requires all inputs to be updated to trigger correctly. If inputs are not properly set, the workflow may halt unexpectedly, especially when using multiple merge nodes in succession.
fix_steps: Add a code node before the second merge node
In the code node, return the value from the previous node using its name instead of $input
Save the workflow and test the execution
agent_instruction: Insert a code node before the second merge node to ensure inputs are correctly updated.
